 The pattern [\\w-]+-Google captures all Google crawlers with \"-Google\" suffix (e.g., Mediapartners-Google, AdsBot-Google, Storebot-Google)\n// as well as crawlers starting with \"Google-\" (e.g., Google-PageRenderer, Google-InspectionTool)\nexport const HTML_LIMITED_BOT_UA_RE = /[\\w-]+-Google|Google-[\\w-]+|Chrome-Lighthouse|Slurp|DuckDuckBot|baiduspider|yandex|sogou|bitlybot|tumblr|vkShare|quora link preview|redditbot|ia_archiver|Bingbot|BingPreview|applebot|facebookexternalhit|facebookcatalog|Twitterbot|LinkedInBot|Slackbot|Discordbot|WhatsApp|SkypeUriPreview|Yeti|googleweblight/i;\n\n//# segment\n    HasLoadingBoundary[HasLoadingBoundary[\"SegmentHasLoadingBoundary\"] = 1] = \"SegmentHasLoadingBoundary\";\n    // There is a loading boundary somewhere in the subtree (but not in\n    // this segment)\n    HasLoadingBoundary[HasLoadingBoundary[\"SubtreeHasLoadingBoundary\"] = 2] = \"SubtreeHasLoadingBoundary\";\n    // There is no loading boundary in this segment or any of its
